title: Alternating series
body:
In mathematics, an alternating series is an infinite series of terms that alternate between positive and negative signs. In capital-sigma notation this is expressed

  
    
      
        
          ∑
          
            n
            =
            0
          
          
            ∞
          
        
        (
        −
        1
        
          )
          
            n
          
        
        
          a
          
            n
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle \sum _{n=0}^{\infty }(-1)^{n}a_{n}}
  
 or 
  
    
      
        
          ∑
          
            n
            =
            0
          
          
            ∞
          
        
        (
        −
        1
        
          )
          
            n
            +
            1
          
        
        
          a
          
            n
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle \sum _{n=0}^{\infty }(-1)^{n+1}a_{n}}
  

with an > 0 for all n.
Like any series, an alternating series is a convergent series if and only if the sequence of partial sums of the series converges to a limit. The alternating series test guarantees that an alternating series is convergent if the terms an converge to 0 monotonically, but this condition is not necessary for convergence.
